content.problemStatement:
Chef has been researching ant colonies for many years and finally discovered all their secrets.

An ant colony consists of NNN distinct lines (numbered 111 through NNN) that pass through a point O\mathsf{O}O, which is the queen's home. For each valid iii, there are MiM_iMiтАЛ ants on the iii-th line.

For each valid iii and jjj, the jjj-th ant on the iii-th line initially has a non-zero coordinate Xi,jX_{i,j}Xi,jтАЛ with the following meaning:


The distance of this ant from O\mathsf{O}O is тИгXi,jтИг|X_{i,j}|тИгXi,jтАЛтИг.
Let's choose a direction along the iii-th line from O\mathsf{O}O. The exact way in which this direction is chosen does not matter here, it only needs to be the same for all ants on the same line.
If Xi,jX_{i,j}Xi,jтАЛ is positive, this ant is at the distance тИгXi,jтИг|X_{i,j}|тИгXi,jтАЛтИг from O\mathsf{O}O in the chosen direction. Otherwise, it is at this distance from O\mathsf{O}O in the opposite direction.
In other words, two ants jjj and kkk on a line iii are at the same side of O\mathsf{O}O if the signs of Xi,jX_{i,j}Xi,jтАЛ and Xi,kX_{i,k}Xi,kтАЛ are the same or on opposite sides if the signs are different.

All ants move with the same constant speed. Initially, all of them are moving towards O\mathsf{O}O. Whenever two or more ants meet (possibly at O\mathsf{O}O), all of these ants turn around and start moving in the opposite directions with the same speed. We call this a collision. Even if an ant reaches O\mathsf{O}O without meeting an ant there, it keeps moving in the same direction. An ant may change direction multiple times.

Help Chef find the total number of collisions between ants. Note that even if more than two ants meet, it counts as only one collision.
constraints: Xi,j<Xi,j+1X_{i,j} \lt X_{i,j+1}Xi,jтАЛ<Xi,j+1тАЛ for each valid i,ji,ji,j
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2тЛЕ1052 \cdot 10^52тЛЕ105
the sum of M1+M2+тАж+MNM_1 + M_2 + \ldots + M_NM1тАЛ+M2тАЛ+тАж+MNтАЛ over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains an integer MiM_iMiтАЛ, followed by a space and MiM_iMiтАЛ space-separated integers Xi,1,Xi,2,тАж,Xi,MiX_{i,1}, X_{i,2}, \ldots, X_{i,M_i}Xi,1тАЛ,Xi,2тАЛ,тАж,Xi,MiтАЛтАЛ.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer тАХ the number of collisions between ants.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: First, the ants on the first line collide at the coordinate тИТ1/2-1/2тИТ1/2 and change directions. Finally, ant 222 on the first line collides with the only ant on the second line; this happens at O\mathsf{O}O. No collisions happen afterwards.
